source: Telegram screenshots (June 17 2026) — 'Add the ticket' returned BTC/ETH prices.
title: Awareness shortcut hijacks imperative commands (no intent gate)
what_failed: try_answer_from_awareness fired on any single keyword match (e.g. 'ticker' in _MARKET_SIGNALS) with no intent gating, so the imperative 'Add the ticker' (autocorrect of 'ticket') returned the cached Markets line and short-circuited BEFORE the LLM/tool loop — the create_ticket request never reached Claude. Ran in BOTH root and normie modes.
where_failed: agent/awareness_shortcut.py::try_answer_from_awareness; enabled via agent/modes.py awareness_shortcut=True for root + normie; dispatched at pi_agent.py:1199-1205.
why_likely: The shortcut was a latency/cost optimization with keyword-only matching; it could not tell a question from a command.
resolution: Part A: added _ACTION_VERBS + _leads_with_action_verb() guard — a message leading with a state-changing verb (add/create/buy/track/...) returns None so the LLM+tools handle it; info verbs (tell/show/give/get) excluded so questions still fire. Part B: disabled awareness_shortcut for root mode (root has tools, rule is 'Act, don't describe'). Tests in test_awareness_shortcut.py.
analysis: Conversation-coherence class: an optimization hijacking real requests. Verified: 'Add the ticker' and 7 other commands now fall through; genuine questions still answered from snapshot.
